name: Prefab Steel House
description:
A prefab steel house from SAMAN is an engineered steel-frame building delivered as a complete 30 × 20 × 13 ft structure — 600 sq ft of usable floor area for ₹6,15,000, built around a welded steel skeleton and clad in insulated panels. It is not a soft-sell "dream home" kit and it is not a finished, furnished villa. It is the building itself: a factory-built steel structure you configure as a home, a live-work space, or a mixed-use block, then put up on your land.

That distinction matters, because most pages ranking for this product blur the line between a steel frame and a turnkey house. SAMAN sells the structure, with the configuration in your hands.

What a 30 × 20 × 13 ft prefab steel house includes at ₹6,15,000

The ₹6,15,000 price is for one specific configuration: a 30 ft × 20 ft footprint, 13 ft to the ridge, 600 sq ft of floor area. That figure buys the full prefab steel frame house as a structure — the welded steel frame, Steel and PUF insulated sandwich wall panels, the roof, one door-and-window arrangement of your choosing, and the factory fabrication of the whole shell. It is a real, comparable number, not a per-square-foot starting rate that climbs once you ask for a quote.

A 600 sq ft prefab steel house set up as a home on a private residential plot.

What sits outside that price is what is genuinely specific to your build: site foundation or plinth work, internal partition walls beyond the base layout, electrical and plumbing fitout, flooring finish, and furniture. We quote those against your drawing so you see each line, rather than folding a vague allowance into the headline figure. For mixed-use and hybrid layouts — a workshop with living quarters, a farm office with a rest room, a site cabin that doubles as accommodation — the base 600 sq ft shell is the same; only the internal configuration changes.

Is a prefab steel house built on a light-gauge or structural steel frame?

A prefab steel house can use either, and the difference decides what the building can become. A light steel prefab house uses thin cold-formed sections — quick to erect, lighter to move, suited to single-storey living spaces. A structural steel frame uses heavier hot-rolled members that carry greater load, support upper floors, and last longer in harsh sites. SAMAN builds to the load your use demands.

The same shell run as a hybrid live-work space — quarters at one end, workshop at the other.

For a single-storey 600 sq ft residential or mixed-use unit, a lighter frame is usually the right specification — it keeps the structure relocatable and the cost controlled. Where the building will carry a mezzanine, heavy internal equipment, or sit on an exposed industrial plot for many years, we step the frame up to heavier structural members. The panels stay the same insulated Steel-and-PUF sandwich either way; what changes is the steel under them. If your decision is really about which grade and coating of steel rather than what the building becomes, that is a separate question — and our dedicated steel shed specification page covers IS-grade steel and coating choices in the detail a procurement file needs.

Steel-frame house, steel shed, or industrial shed — which your project needs

Buyers reach this page after searching three different things that look alike. A steel structure prefab house, a steel shed, and an industrial shed are built from the same family of steel but serve different ends, and choosing the wrong one means paying for capacity you will not use or specifying a building you cannot live in.

Product Built for Choose it when
Prefab steel house Habitable / mixed-use building You will live, work, or run a hybrid use inside it
Steel shed Covered storage or work bay The space holds goods, vehicles, or a workshop — not people full-time
Industrial shed Large-span factory / warehouse You need clear-span area over a factory floor or logistics operation

A farm-office-with-quarters configuration of the steel-frame house on agricultural land.

In short: if people occupy the space day to day, you want the prefab steel house. If you are covering material or machinery, a steel shed is cheaper and correct. If you are spanning a factory floor, that is hub-level industrial shed territory.

Foundation, delivery, and on-site assembly before you move in

A prefab steel house needs a level base. For most 600 sq ft units a simple concrete plinth or a compacted, levelled footing is enough — your civil contractor handles that, and we share the foundation drawing and bolt-down points once your layout is confirmed. The structure does not require deep RCC foundations the way a brick-and-mortar house does, which is a large part of why off-site steel construction goes up faster.

On-site assembly on a level plinth — frame up, insulated panels going on, window openings framed.

Fabrication of the shell takes place in our factory; the unit is then delivered and assembled on your site. Site assembly of a single 600 sq ft steel structure is a matter of days, not months, once the base is ready and access is clear. Because the frame is bolted and the panels are mechanically fixed, a steel-frame building can also be dismantled and relocated later — useful for leased land, phased projects, or anyone who expects the structure to move with them.

Buying direct from SAMAN's industrial shed range

The prefab steel house sits within SAMAN's wider steel-building line. Buying factory-direct means the people who fabricate the frame are the people who quote it, drawing on the same IS-grade steel and panel stock used across the range — so the structure you specify is the structure that is built and delivered. To see how this unit fits alongside the other steel structures and to compare specifications across the range, start from our industrial sheds collection.

The steel-frame house on a peri-urban plot — a buy-direct, configured building ready to occupy.

Tell us your land size, intended use, and whether the build is single-storey or carries an upper floor, and we will return a configured quote against the ₹6,15,000 base.

Get a configured quote

Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
short_description:
600 sq ft engineered steel-frame building, configured for you

Factory-built prefab steel house on a welded steel frame with insulated Steel & PUF panels — for residential, live-work, or mixed-use builds.

Size / Capacity
30×20×13 ft / 600 sq ft
Frame / Panel
Steel frame / PUF panel
Price / Delivery
₹6,15,000 / days
Frame Type / Relocatable
Light or structural / Yes
Factory-direct, configured to use
From ₹6,15,000
